title: Interest groups influencing policymaking
description: Test your knowledge on the potential benefits and drawbacks of interest group influence on policymaking.

assessment_items.question_content:
Club for Growth, a major interest group, recruits successful businessman, Tony Stark, to run for Senate in New York. They endorse him on their group’s website, organize voter drives in New York City, and fundraise through their Political Action Committee (PAC).

**Which of the following statements best describes what this scenario illustrates?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: how interest groups take part in campaigns to get people with similar values elected
how interest groups influence office holders to pass policies that match their policy goals
how interest groups use polling data to convince potential candidates to run for office
how interest groups work with government agencies to influence the implementation of policy

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following scenarios best illustrates a way in which an interest group can influence policymaking?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The United Auto Workers encourage their members to reach out to their representatives to support a jobs bill.
League of Women Voters publishes a pamphlet that includes the policy positions of all candidates running for the presidency.
The Department of Transportation issues a new rule about speed limits on national highways after the president signs a bill into law.
The president’s reelection committee polls the American public to determine whether they have the support they need to win.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following scenarios best illustrates a way in which an interest group can influence policymaking?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Clean Water Action pays for a television advertisement that highlights the potential outcomes of a new piece of green legislation.
The Republican Party hosts a party convention to determine their candidate for the 2020 presidential election.
The announcer on a major news network hosts a televised debate between candidates for the 2020 presidential election.
Mothers Against Drunk Driving holds a victim impact panel for people convicted of driving while under the influence of alcohol.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following scenarios best illustrates a way in which an interest group can influence policymaking?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The AFL-CIO issues a statement that their organization supports a bill improving working conditions for checkout clerks.
The head of the National Organization for Women gives an interview about the history of the organization to a major network.
The Green Party petitions to get their candidate on the 2020 presidential ballot in several states to give their candidate a platform.
The House committee on Science, Space, and Technology request the testimonies of NASA leadership members.

assessment_items.question_content:
The Department of Education, the American Federation of Teachers, and the House Committee on Education and Labor work together to improve public education outcomes.

**This scenario illustrates which of the following concepts?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: iron triangles
political action committees
third parties
political realignment

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best illustrates the concept of issue networks?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the open system of knowledgeable groups and individuals who promote competing policy perspectives
the longstanding bonds between interest groups, congressional committees, and agencies in particular policy areas
the problem when a person not involved in an organization benefits from their fight for a collective good
a formal organization in which people join and seek to influence public policy without taking on roles in government

assessment_items.question_content:
The National Farmers Union has been encouraging its members to pressure their representatives to support a bill to help address farm debt. Although Farmer Daniela cares about addressing farm debt, she does not want to call her representative. Even without Daniela’s work, the bill gets the support it needs to pass the House.

**Which of the following statements best describes what this scenario illustrates?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the free-rider problem of getting support for a collective good
the advantage that incumbents have when running for office
the belief that your vote matters and can influence the outcomes of elections
the balance of protecting minority rights from majority rule

assessment_items.question_content:
Even though he just retired, Bill sees no reason to join the American Association of Retired Persons (AARP), as the dues are not enough to make much difference in protecting his health care, and he expects that he will benefit from the association’s activities whether he becomes a member or not. 

**This scenario reflects which of the following political concepts?
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Free-rider problem
Friendly incumbent rule
Equal access opportunity
Issue network influence

assessment_items.question_content:
####Use the table to answer the question below.

Lobbying client | Total spent in 2018 | Total spent in 2019
- | - | -
**US Chamber of Congress** | $\$94,800,000$ | $\$77,245,000$
**National Association of Realtors** | $\$72,808,648$ | $\$41,241,006$
**Amazon.com** | $\$14,400,000$ | $\$16,790,000$
**Comcast Corp** | $\$15,072,000$ | $\$13,360,000$
**AT&T Inc** | $\$18,529,000$ | $\$12,820,000$

**Given the information in the table, which of the following groups has the most financial resources to use to influence policymaking?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The US Chamber of Congress
National Association of Realtors
Amazon.com
Comcast Corp

assessment_items.question_content:
####Use the table to answer the question below.

Lobbying client | Total spent in 2018 | Total spent in 2019
- | - | -
**US Chamber of Congress** | $\$94,800,000$ | $\$77,245,000$
**National Association of Realtors** | $\$72,808,648$ | $\$41,241,006$
**Amazon.com** | $\$14,400,000$ | $\$16,790,000$
**Comcast Corp** | $\$15,072,000$ | $\$13,360,000$
**AT&T Inc** | $\$18,529,000$ | $\$12,820,000$

**The data in the table best illustrate which of the following common criticisms of the role of interest groups in politics?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Not all interest groups have the same economic resources, giving some groups more access to decision makers.
Despite not all interest groups having the same access, no interest group dominates political discussions.
Elite professionals who do not care about the needs of the common American staff interest groups.
Interest groups have no accountability to the people, even though they have significant influence in politics.

assessment_items.question_content:
Two groups are attempting to influence whether a gambling bill makes it through Congress. One group representing online poker champions opposed, while the other group representing the major sports leagues like the National Basketball Association and the National Football League supported the bill. 

**A critic of the influence of interest groups would make which of the following claims?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The major sports league interest group has greater access to decision makers than the online poker players interest group leading to Congress to support their position.
The outcome of this bill directly affects the poker players, but not the sports leagues, so they should not be able to influence members of Congress to support their position.
Members of Congress are beholden to people, not organizations, so they will be more likely to support the online poker champions group as the other group is made up of major associations.
The interest groups will use political advertisements to state their position to the public and encourage individual citizens to pressure their representatives to either support or oppose the bill.

assessment_items.question_content:
Two groups are attempting to influence policy. One group represents the interests of a major shipping company. The other group represents the workers responsible for shipping the product. 

**A critic of the influence of interest groups would make which of the following claims?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The shipping company group can use their vast resources to politically and economically influence politicians, although the second group may represent more people.
The interest groups can sway politicians to their side through a multitude of means, including providing policy research, going public, and using the court system.
The workers' group will face the free-rider problem if they can get any solutions to their problems because the government cannot provide benefits to workers only involved in the group.
The interest groups will rely on using public opinion data to sway politicians to their side of the issue or the politicians will face losing reelection when their terms end.
